name: Ascend CLI Interface
description: Command-line interface for Ascend AI-powered engineering management tool. Implements flat subcommand architecture with argument rewriting to support both space-separated and hyphenated command styles. Provides 50+ commands for roster management, team operations, meeting analysis, sync integrations, reporting, planning, coaching, and scheduling.

types.name: ArgumentParser
types.description: argparse.ArgumentParser type - standard library parser for CLI arguments

functions.name: _build_parser
functions.description: Constructs the complete ArgumentParser with all 50+ subcommands for the Ascend CLI. Defines the entire command hierarchy including init, doctor, config, roster, team, meeting, sync, report, plan, coach, schedule, and tui subcommands with their respective arguments and flags.
preconditions: ascend.__version__ must be defined and accessible
postconditions: Returns a fully configured ArgumentParser with prog='ascend'
All 50+ subcommands are registered with their arguments
Each subcommand with optional --json flag for JSON output
Mutually exclusive groups configured for schedule-add subcommand

functions.name: _rewrite_args
functions.description: Rewrites space-separated command arguments to hyphenated format. Transforms commands like 'roster list' to 'roster-list' and 'plan goal create' to 'plan-goal-create'. Tries longest match first (3-word commands, then 2-word commands).

inputs.name: argv
inputs.description: List of command-line arguments to rewrite

preconditions: argv is a list of strings
postconditions: Returns rewritten argument list with space-separated commands converted to hyphenated format
If no match found, returns original argv unchanged
Remaining arguments after matched command are preserved

functions.name: main
functions.description: CLI entry point that parses arguments, rewrites command syntax, and dispatches to appropriate command handlers. Uses lazy imports to keep startup fast. Exits with code 1 if no command provided or command not recognized.

inputs.name: argv
inputs.description: Optional list of command-line arguments. If None, uses sys.argv

error_cases.name: UnrecognizedCommand
error_cases.condition: args.command does not match any elif branch

preconditions: If argv is provided, must be a list of strings or None
postconditions: If no command provided, prints help and exits with code 1
If unrecognized command, prints help and exits with code 1
If valid command, imports and executes corresponding command handler
Command handlers are imported lazily only when needed
side_effects: Calls sys.exit(1) if no command or invalid command
Prints help message to stdout via parser.print_help()
Executes command handler functions with parsed arguments

structured_side_effects.description: Exits process with code 1 on errors

invariants: All subcommands are registered with unique names in hyphenated format
Command rewriting preserves argument order and extra flags
Parser always has a --version flag at top level
The 'command' destination is set via dest='command' on subparsers
Exit code is always 1 for help/error conditions
Module uses __future__ annotations for forward compatibility
